content.problemStatement:
Utkarsh is forced to play yet another game with Ashish.

In this game there are NNN piles, ithi^{th}ith pile contains AiA_iAi​ stones. **Utkarsh moves first**.

In Utkarsh's turn, Ashish chooses a pile (which contains at least 111 stone), then Utkarsh removes any non-zero number of stones from that pile. Similarly, in Ashish's turn, Utkarsh chooses a pile with at least 111 stone, then Ashish removes any non-zero number of stones from that pile. The player who makes the last move wins.

Determine the winner of the game if both players play optimally.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the number of piles.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, where AiA_iAi​ denotes the number of stones in the ithi^{th}ith pile.


Output Format
For each test case, output on one line `Utkarsh` if Utkarsh wins the game, else output `Ashish`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `Utkarsh`, `utkarsh`, `UTKARSH` and `uTkArSh` will all be treated as identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Since there is only 111 pile, Ashish can choose only that pile for Utkarsh and he will remove all the stones from that pile and thus win the game.
Test case 222: No matter which player choose which pile, the game will run for exactly 444 turns and thus Ashish will win the game.
